engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler Grand Voyager in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chrysler Grand Voyager VM64C engine - diesel 2.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger failure - variable geometry vanes stick, causing loss of power and limp mode.
High-pressure fuel pump wear - leads to hard starting and injector damage over time.

cards.altText: Chrysler Grand Voyager EGA engine - petrol 3.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Intake manifold gasket leaks - causes vacuum leaks, rough idling, and occasional misfires.
Oil sludge buildup - if maintenance is delayed, leading to premature bearing wear.

cards.altText: Chrysler Grand Voyager EDZ engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - causes rattling noise on startup and potential valve timing issues.
Oil consumption - worn piston rings lead to increased oil usage and smoke.

engineGuide.h2: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chrysler Grand Voyager across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: The definitive powerplant for the UK Mk5 Grand Voyager, offering strong torque but requiring diligent maintenance to avoid turbo and injector issues.
commonFailures: Turbocharger VGT actuator failure causing limp mode.
High-pressure fuel pump wear leading to hard starting.
entries.cta: Get quotes for VM64C Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: An alternative designation for the 2.8 CRD VM Motori engine, sharing identical specifications and failure patterns with the VM64C.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging leading to restricted airflow and power loss.
Injector seal failure causing oil contamination.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENS Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The robust VM Motori workhorse of the Mk4 generation, known for simplicity and durability when serviced regularly.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear if service intervals are exceeded.
Injector nozzle coking causing rough idle and smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204D4 Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: The modern, highly reliable V6 that replaced older designs, featuring dual overhead cams and improved fuel efficiency for the Mk5 range.
commonFailures: Oil filter housing gasket leaks causing oil loss.
Timing phaser rattle on cold starts due to worn components.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

entries.title: 3.3L Petrol
entries.history: The standard, smooth-running V6 for the Mk4 generation, offering ample power for the heavy MPV body with minimal complexity.
commonFailures: Intake manifold gasket degradation causing vacuum leaks.
Oil sludge accumulation from delayed oil changes.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGA Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Part of the Global Engine Manufacturing Alliance (GEMA), this 4-cylinder unit provides adequate power but requires strict timing chain maintenance.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch causing startup rattling and misfires.
High oil consumption due to worn piston rings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EDZ Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Grand Voyager is predominantly affected by issues within its 2.8 CRD diesel and 2.4L/3.3L petrol engine families, particularly concerning turbochargers, timing components, and oiling systems. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD Turbo Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD Turbo Failure - Why VM64C Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Grand Voyager SE, Executive, Limited (2008-2014, VM64C/ENS)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup in the variable geometry turbocharger vanes restricts movement, causing boost leaks, limp mode, and eventual turbo seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cleaning vanes and replacing the electronic actuator.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if the turbo core is undamaged and housing is not cracked.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a reconditioned unit with a new turbo and injectors.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; restores reliability with a 12-month warranty.

problems.recommendation: If the turbo has seized and sent metal shards into the intercooler or engine, a full rebuilt replacement is vastly superior to a standalone turbo swap, which often fails again due to underlying oil starvation.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt VM64C engine prices for your Chrysler Grand Voyager

problems.group: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.4L Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.4L Timing Chain Failure - Why EDZ Engines Rattle
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner wears out, allowing the chain to stretch and slap against the guides, leading to skipped teeth and catastrophic valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the chain, guides, tensioner, and sprockets.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only if caught early before valve-to-piston contact occurs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a reconditioned 2.4L unit with upgraded timing components.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if the engine has already suffered timing-related internal damage.

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of a cold-start rattle, investigate immediately. If the chain has already jumped, a reconditioned engine is the most cost-effective and reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Find reconditioned EDZ engine options for your Chrysler Grand Voyager

problems.group: Chrysler Grand Voyager 3.3L Oil Sludge & Intake Leaks
problems.h4: Chrysler Grand Voyager 3.3L Oil Sludge & Intake Leaks - Cooling System Weaknesses
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Grand Voyager SE, Executive (2001-2007, EGA)
problems.rootCause: Delayed oil changes cause sludge to block oil galleries, while the plastic intake manifold gaskets degrade, causing vacuum leaks and rough idling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing intake gaskets and performing a chemical engine flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly cost-effective if the engine block shows no signs of bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a fully remanufactured 3.3L V6 unit.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage units to ensure long-term reliability.

problems.recommendation: For a vehicle of this age, if sludge is confirmed via an oil cap inspection, investing in a remanufactured engine ensures factory-spec longevity and prevents recurrent overheating or bearing issues.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Grand Voyager 3.3L engine replacement

problems.group: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.rootCause: Coolant degradation, aging plastic thermostat housings, and water pump failures lead to localized overheating and head gasket blowout.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the cylinder head, machining, and fitting new gaskets.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only if the alloy block and head are not warped by extreme heat.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit of a fully reconditioned engine with a new cooling system.
repairOptions.longevity: The safest route to guarantee reliability and prevent recurring overheating.

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repairs on high-mileage Grand Voyagers are risky. A reconditioned engine with a pressure-tested block and new water pump is the definitive, long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare prices for Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Grand Voyager
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failure does not have to mean the end of your vehicle. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Chrysler Grand Voyager specialists, ensuring you get a fair deal without the main dealer premium. Every replacement engine features upgraded components addressing the original factory flaws, backed by a comprehensive 12-month warranty and nationwide delivery.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Grand Voyager and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The original diesel workhorse for the Mk4 Grand Voyager, known for its simplicity and robust VM Motori construction. While less powerful than later iterations, it remains a highly durable and cost-effective option for older models.
commonFailurePoints: injector nozzle coking at high mileage.

items.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced for the Mk5 generation, the 2.8 CRD became the most common Grand Voyager engine in the UK. While offering strong torque, its variable geometry turbocharger and high-pressure fuel system require diligent maintenance to avoid limp mode.

groups.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager 2.4L Petrol Engines

commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch causing startup rattling

items.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager 3.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The standard, smooth-running V6 for the Mk4 generation, offering ample power for the Grand Voyager with minimal complexity. It is a robust unit that responds well to reconditioning when oil sludge becomes an issue.

items.title: Chrysler Grand Voyager 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern, highly reliable Pentastar V6 that replaced older designs for the late Mk5 range. It features dual overhead cams and improved fuel efficiency, making it a sought-after, durable replacement option.
commonFailurePoints: timing phaser rattle on cold starts.

knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability.
Severe turbocharger VGT sticking and DPF issues if used only for short journeys.
High UK replacement demand for the 2.8L CRD due to age and specific factory flaws.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Chrysler Grand Voyager's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The launch of the RS-chassis Mk4 Grand Voyager introduced a more refined, car-like driving experience with robust V6 and VM Motori diesel options.
keyChanges: Introduction of the new RS platform with independent rear suspension.
Exclusive use of naturally aspirated V6 petrol and VM Motori diesel engines.
Distinctive styling setting it apart from the previous generation.
knownFor: Robust and simple engine design, especially the 3.3L V6.
Timing chain stretch on the 2.4L EDZ at high mileage.

faq.h2: Chrysler Grand Voyager Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement typically costs between £1,500 and £3,000 in the UK market. This price range depends heavily on your specific engine code, the chosen condition of the unit, and whether you require a supply-only option or a complete supply-and-fit package. Budget-friendly used options start lower, while premium remanufactured 3.6L Pentastar units reach the higher end of this spectrum.
keyPoints: 2.8 CRD rebuilt engines start around £1,800 (supply only).
3.6L V6 remanufactured units can reach £2,800.
Supply and fit adds approximately £600 to £1,200 in labour.
items.cta: Get a free, no-obligation Chrysler Grand Voyager engine quote today

items.question: What is the most common engine failure in a Chrysler Grand Voyager?
items.answer: The most common failure is turbocharger and high-pressure fuel pump issues in the 2.8 CRD (VM64C) diesel engine, leading to loss of power and limp mode. This affects 2008-2014 models predominantly and is caused by carbon buildup and delayed maintenance.
keyPoints: Affects 2008-2014 models predominantly.
Caused by VGT turbo actuator sticking and injector wear.
Results in severe performance loss and potential engine starvation.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt VM64C engine prices for your Chrysler Grand Voyager

items.question: Can I get a Chrysler Grand Voyager engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Yes, our network includes vetted, specialist UK garages experienced in fitting American MPV engines, offering a complete hassle-free solution. Mainstream garages often refuse complex American-spec vehicles, making our specialist network essential for a seamless installation.
keyPoints: Mainstream garages often refuse American-spec vehicles.
Our partners are specifically trained on Chrysler V6 and CRD architectures.
The service includes ECU compatibility checks and fluid top-ups.
items.cta: Book your Chrysler Grand Voyager engine supply and fit service now

items.question: Are rebuilt Chrysler Grand Voyager engines reliable?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt Chrysler Grand Voyager engines are highly reliable, often exceeding original factory durability due to the remediation of known design flaws during the rebuild process, such as upgraded turbos and timing components.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 2.8 CRD units feature upgraded turbochargers.
New timing chains, guides, and tensioners are installed on petrol models.
All units undergo rigorous compression and leak-down testing.
items.cta: View refurbished and rebuilt Chrysler Grand Voyager engine options

items.question: How long does a Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement take?
items.answer: Once the engine is sourced, the physical supply and fit process typically takes 1 to 3 days, depending on the specialist garage's schedule. Sourcing a specific engine code like the VM64C may take 2-5 days if not immediately in local stock.
keyPoints: Sourcing a specific engine code may take 2-5 days.
UK-wide courier delivery is rapid and tracked.
Complex swaps may require slight ECU adaptations or loom checks.
items.cta: Find rapid turnaround Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement deals

items.question: Do Chrysler Grand Voyager replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through our network includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering both parts and labour. This provides complete peace of mind for your investment.
keyPoints: Protects your investment against future manufacturing defects.
Premium trims often qualify for extended warranty options.
Warranty is valid at any of our approved specialist fitting centres.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Grand Voyager engine prices with full warranty included

items.question: Can I replace my 2.4L Grand Voyager engine with a 3.3L or 3.6L?
items.answer: While physically possible, upgrading from a 2.4L to a V6 requires ECU reprogramming, wiring harness modifications, and potentially a transmission swap to handle the increased torque.
keyPoints: The V6 is heavier and requires specific engine mounts.
Transmission shift points must be recalibrated for the larger engine.
Our specialist network can advise on feasibility and custom costs.
items.cta: Speak to our Chrysler Grand Voyager engine specialists for upgrade advice

items.question: How do I find the correct engine code for my Chrysler Grand Voyager?
items.answer: You can find your engine code by checking the vehicle's VIN plate under the bonnet, or looking at the stamping on the engine block itself near the gearbox bell housing.
keyPoints: 'VM64C' or 'ENS' indicates the 2.8L CRD diesel engine.
'EGA' indicates the 3.3L V6 petrol engine.
'ERB' indicates the 3.6L Pentastar petrol engine.
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly verify your Chrysler Grand Voyager engine code

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Chrysler Grand Voyager?
items.answer: Yes, if the bodywork, transmission, and interior are in good condition, a warrantied replacement engine is a highly cost-effective way to extend the vehicle's life by years, avoiding the high cost of a newer MPV.
keyPoints: Cheaper than purchasing a new, depreciating vehicle outright.
Rebuilt engines reset the mileage clock on the powertrain.
Maintains the unique, spacious driving dynamics of the Grand Voyager.
items.cta: Check if your Chrysler Grand Voyager engine replacement is economically viable

items.question: Where can I buy a used Chrysler Grand Voyager engine near me?
items.answer: While local physical stock is rare, our UK-wide network and courier service can deliver a thoroughly tested, warrantied used engine directly to your chosen specialist garage, ensuring you get the best price.
keyPoints: We aggregate stock from national American car specialists.
All used engines undergo strict compression and oil analysis.
Nationwide delivery ensures you get the best price, not just the closest.
